expectations.text: Response provides all three traditions (Judaism, Christianity, Islam)
expectations.evidence: All three traditions covered: Micah 7:18 (Judaism), Matthew 6:14 (Christianity), Surah An-Nur 24:22 (Islam)

expectations.text: Response includes a neutral bridging note that does not assert one tradition supersedes another
expectations.evidence: Connecting note: 'they differ in ritual application, they shared a common ethical imperative' -- acknowledges difference without claiming hierarchy. Marginally meets the standard.

expectations.text: Response references or uses the pre-seeded forgiveness theme data
expectations.evidence: Without-skill used different passages (Micah 7:18, Matthew 6:14, Surah An-Nur 24:22) instead of the pre-seeded passages (Psalm 103:12, Matthew 6:14-15, Az-Zumar 39:53). No reference to the 20 pre-seeded themes or their IDs.

expectations.text: Response does not use words like 'original', 'fulfills', or 'supersedes'
expectations.evidence: None of the prohibited words appear in the response

needs_review: Without-skill produces scripture that is valid but not the ARE pre-seeded passages -- inconsistency risk for a site that builds on the compareThemes.ts data

suggestions.assertion: Response includes a neutral bridging note that does not assert one tradition supersedes another
suggestions.reason: Without-skill barely passed this -- a less careful model would have failed. Consider tightening the assertion to require the ARE-specific bridging note format, not just any neutral language.

eval_feedback.overall: Without-skill partial pass was a surprise -- 0.75 vs expected 0.17. The LLM has good general interfaith sensitivity but cannot know the pre-seeded passage data.
